claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with a few basic questions:

1. **Do you have a child or children you're responsible for?** If yes, how many children are you asking about, and what are their names and ages?

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. My son Alex is 20 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and lives at home with you.

Since Alex is 20, I need to ask some important follow-up questions, as Child Benefit eligibility for children over 16 depends on specific circumstances:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

For example, is he enrolled in full-time non-advanced education (like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQ level 3 or below) or an approved training program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school a while back and isn't studying anything right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that information.

Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**I'm sorry, but you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for:
- Children under 16 years old, **or**
- Young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training

Since Alex is 20 years old, he is over the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility. Even if he were in approved education or training, the benefit stops once a young person reaches 20 (or sometimes turns 20, depending on when their courses end).

Child Benefit would have stopped being payable for Alex when he either:
- Left education/training after age 16, or
- Reached the age of 20 (whichever came first)

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old, he is over the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, even if he were in approved education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those aged 16-19 in approved education or training. As Alex is 20, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.

Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim Child Benefit for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old, he is over the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, even if he were in approved education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those aged 16-19 in approved education or training. As Alex is 20, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.
